classObjective: Identify and classify three-dimensional figures as polyhedra or non-polyhedra.
workedExamples: 11-5, Example 1 — Identify and Classify Three-Dimensional Figures
11-5, Example 2 — Model Real-World Objects with Three-Dimensional Figures
warmUp: What is the difference between a polyhedron and a non-polyhedron?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: polyhedra vs. cylinders/cones/spheres; naming faces, edges, vertices; Euler's formula introduction
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice identifying and classifying 3D figures
independentPractice: Practice problems identifying 3D figures and classifying as polyhedra or non-polyhedra
assessment: Exit ticket: classify a cylinder, cone, and rectangular prism as polyhedron or non-polyhedron and explain why
capReflection: Why is a sphere a non-polyhedron even though it's a common 3D shape?
